Home » Hearing » Understanding Swimmer’s Ear: Causes, Symptoms, and Treatment Options

Understanding Swimmer’s Ear: Causes, Symptoms, and Treatment Options

  • Debra Adair, HAS
  • December 10, 2025

Swimmer’s ear is a common ear problem, especially in warm places like Florida. This guide breaks down what it is, how it starts, and how Florida Medical Hearing Aids in Sebring and Lake Placid can help you get relief and avoid future infections.

Many people in Florida enjoy spending time in pools, lakes, and oceans. But this also means many people deal with swimmer’s ear, a painful ear problem that can happen when water gets trapped inside the ear canal. Swimmer’s ear can affect both children and adults, and it often gets worse if it is not treated quickly. Learning what causes it and how to treat it can help you avoid pain and protect your hearing.

Swimmer’s ear is an infection that happens in the outer ear canal. The canal is the small tunnel that leads from the outside of your ear to your eardrum. When this area becomes warm and moist, bacteria can grow easily. Once bacteria begin to grow, the skin inside the ear can become red, swollen, and painful. Even a small amount of water left in the ear after swimming, showering, or bathing can sometimes cause swimmer’s ear.

At Florida Medical Hearing Aids, we help many people who come in with questions about ear pain, pressure, or sudden discomfort after being in the water. Understanding the signs and knowing when to get help can make a big difference in healing faster and preventing future infections. In this article, you will learn what swimmer’s ear feels like, what causes it, how it is treated, and how you can keep your ears safe all year long.

What Is Swimmer’s Ear?

Swimmer’s ear, also called otitis externa, is an infection of the skin in the outer ear canal. This is different from a middle ear infection, which happens deeper inside behind the eardrum. Swimmer’s ear affects the part of the ear you can touch with your finger (though you should never put anything inside the ear), and the infection can spread if it is not treated.

The outer ear canal is warm, dark, and can stay wet after being in water. These conditions help germs grow quickly. Once germs grow, the skin inside the ear becomes irritated and sore. The infection can range from mild to severe, depending on how long it has been there and how much the skin has swollen.

Common Symptoms of Swimmer’s Ear

Swimmer’s ear usually begins with mild signs. These symptoms can get worse over time, especially if the ear is scratched, cleaned too deeply, or left wet.

Common symptoms include:

  • Itching inside the ear
  • Redness around the ear opening
  • A feeling of fullness or pressure
  • Ear pain that gets worse when you pull the ear or push on the outer ear
  • Fluid or pus draining from the ear
  • Trouble hearing clearly due to swelling
  • A plugged or blocked feeling
  • Tenderness around the ear

If the infection becomes more serious, symptoms may include:

  • Strong pain
  • Fever
  • Swollen lymph nodes near the ear
  • Thick drainage

If you notice these stronger symptoms, it is important to get help right away so the infection does not spread or cause more problems.

What Causes Swimmer’s Ear?

Swimmer’s ear can happen for many reasons, even if you have not been swimming recently. Water is the most common cause, but other things can irritate the ear canal and lead to infection.

1. Water Trapped in the Ear

This is the most common cause. Water can stay stuck in the ear after:

  • Swimming
  • Taking a shower
  • Bathing
  • Sweating heavily

When water stays in the canal, it softens the skin. This makes it easier for bacteria to grow.

2. Scratching or Cleaning the Ear Too Deeply

Using cotton swabs, fingernails, or other objects can damage the ear canal. Even a tiny scratch can allow germs to enter and cause infection.

3. Allergies or Skin Problems

People with eczema, psoriasis, or allergies may have dry or flaky skin in the ear. Dry, cracked skin makes infection more likely.

4. Earplugs or Hearing Aids That Fit Too Tight

Anything that rubs the inside of the ear canal can irritate the skin. When the skin becomes irritated, germs can enter easily.

5. Dirt or Sand in the Ear

Florida beaches are beautiful, but sand and dirt can sometimes enter the ears. This can irritate the ear canal and lead to swimmer’s ear.

How Swimmer’s Ear Is Diagnosed

A hearing specialist or doctor can diagnose swimmer’s ear with a simple ear exam. At Florida Medical Hearing Aids, we check the outer ear canal with a tool that lets us see inside. We look for:

  • Redness
  • Swelling
  • Fluid
  • Blockages
  • Skin changes

We may also check your hearing if you feel muffled or blocked, because swelling can affect how sound moves through the ear.

If the infection is severe, we may recommend medical treatment or a visit with a doctor for prescription drops.

How Swimmer’s Ear Is Treated

Treatment depends on how strong the infection is. Some mild cases can be managed at home, but many need medicine to stop the bacteria from growing.

1. Ear Drops

Prescription ear drops are the most common treatment. These drops may include:

  • Antibiotics to kill bacteria
  • Steroids to reduce swelling
  • Pain relievers

They are placed directly in the ear canal so the medicine can reach the infection.

2. Cleaning the Ear Canal

A healthcare provider may clean the ear canal to remove debris, wax, or drainage. This helps the ear drops work better.

3. Pain Relief

Over-the-counter pain medicine can help reduce discomfort while the infection heals.

4. Avoiding Water

Until the infection clears, you should keep the ear dry. This means avoiding swimming and being careful in the shower.

5. Follow-Up Care

Some people need follow-up visits to make sure the infection is improving, especially if symptoms were severe.

It is important not to try to treat swimmer’s ear with home remedies like vinegar or hydrogen peroxide without guidance. These can make the infection worse if used incorrectly.

Home Care Tips for Swimmer’s Ear

Some steps can help you feel better while the infection heals:

  • Use warm compresses to reduce pain
  • Sit upright to help drainage
  • Keep the ear dry during showers
  • Avoid inserting anything into the ear
  • Take medicine exactly as directed

Swimmer’s ear often heals quickly when treated the right way.

How to Prevent Swimmer’s Ear

Prevention is the best way to avoid ear pain and infections.

1. Keep Ears Dry

After swimming or showering, gently dry your ears with a towel. Tilt your head to each side to help water escape.

2. Use Earplugs Safely

If you swim often, use earplugs made for water protection. Choose soft plugs that fit well but do not push too deep.

3. Avoid Cotton Swabs

Cotton swabs can push wax deeper and scratch the ear canal. This increases the risk of infection.

4. Protect Your Ears at the Beach

If you’re around sand or dirt, be careful not to let debris get inside your ears.

5. Treat Skin Conditions

If you have eczema or allergies, talk to your healthcare provider about how to keep the skin in your ears healthy.

6. Use Drops After Swimming

Some people use over-the-counter drying drops after swimming. Ask a specialist if these are safe for you. Florida Medical Hearing connects you to an expert hearing aid specialist in Sebring, FL.

Swimmer’s Ear in Children

Kids often get swimmer’s ear because their ear canals are smaller, making it easier for water to get trapped. They may complain of:

  • Ear pain
  • Itching
  • Trouble hearing
  • Fluid coming out of the ear

Children may also tug on the ear or cry when lying down. If your child has these symptoms, a visit with a specialist can help them feel better quickly.

When to See a Hearing Specialist

You should get help if:

  • Ear pain lasts more than 24–48 hours
  • You notice drainage or foul smell
  • Hearing becomes muffled or blocked
  • The ear is very swollen or tender
  • You develop fever
  • You get swimmer’s ear often

At Florida Medical Hearing Aids, we can check your ears, recommend treatment, and help you avoid future infections, especially if you live an active lifestyle or spend time in the water.

Complications of Untreated Swimmer’s Ear

If swimmer’s ear is not treated, the infection can get worse. Possible complications include:

  • Severe pain
  • Swelling that closes the ear canal
  • Long-term infections
  • Spread of bacteria to nearby areas
  • Temporary hearing loss

Most problems can be prevented with early care.

Swimmer’s Ear vs. Middle Ear Infection

It is important to know that swimmer’s ear is different from a middle ear infection.

Swimmer’s ear affects:

  • The outer ear canal

Middle ear infections affect:

  • The space behind the eardrum

Middle ear infections often cause:

  • Fever
  • Trouble sleeping
  • Pain when lying down
  • Less ear tenderness on the outside

Swimmer’s ear usually causes more pain when you touch the outer ear. A specialist can help you know which type you have.

How Florida Medical Hearing Aids Can Help

With locations in Sebring and Lake Placid, Florida, our team cares for people of all ages. We understand how painful and frustrating swimmer’s ear can be, especially in a warm, active state like Florida where water activities are a big part of daily life.

We provide:

  • Ear checks
  • Ear cleaning
  • Help with ear health questions
  • Advice for water protection
  • Guidance on hearing changes
  • Support for long-term ear care

Our goal is to help you feel better quickly and keep future infections away.

Frequently Asked Questions

Can swimmer’s ear be prevented?

Yes. Swimmer’s ear can often be prevented with a few simple habits. Keeping your ears dry after swimming or showering is one of the best ways to lower your risk. You can gently tilt your head to each side to help water drain out, and pat the outer ear with a towel. Avoid using cotton swabs or putting objects inside the ear, because this can scratch the skin and make infections more likely. People who swim often can also use well-fitting swim earplugs or ask a specialist about safe drying drops. Keeping the ears clean, dry, and protected is the easiest way to prevent swimmer’s ear.

Is swimmer’s ear contagious?

No. Swimmer’s ear is not contagious and cannot spread from one person to another. It is an infection that happens inside the outer ear canal, usually because water, bacteria, or irritation caused the skin to break down. Even if someone in your home has swimmer’s ear, you cannot “catch” it from them. Each case develops from conditions inside the person’s own ear, not from contact with others.

How long does swimmer’s ear last?

The length of swimmer’s ear depends on how early it is treated. Mild cases can start to improve within a few days once proper care or ear drops are used. More serious infections may take a week or longer to fully clear. If the ear canal is very swollen or painful, healing can take more time. It is important to follow all treatment steps and keep the ear dry while it heals. If symptoms do not improve after 48–72 hours of treatment, or if the pain gets worse, a hearing specialist or doctor should check the ear again to make sure the infection is healing the way it should.

Final Thoughts

Swimmer’s ear is a common and painful problem that affects people of all ages, especially those who spend time in pools, lakes, or oceans. The infection starts when water or irritation allows germs to grow in the outer ear canal. While it can be painful, swimmer’s ear is usually easy to treat when you get help early. Keeping your ears dry, avoiding deep cleaning, and protecting them from irritation can help you avoid future infections. At Florida Medical Hearing Aids in Sebring and Lake Placid, we are here to help you keep your ears healthy and pain-free, especially if you are dealing with swimmer’s ear or want to prevent it from returning.

Hearing Test Online: What You Need to Know

  • Debra Adair, HAS
  • October 13, 2025

Many people wonder if they can check their hearing from home with an online test. It sounds easy and convenient, especially if you are starting to notice small changes in the way you hear. Maybe you have trouble following conversations in noisy places, or you turn the TV up louder than before. An online test can seem like a quick way to get answers. But is it enough?

At Florida Medical Hearing, we understand how important it is to take that first step. While online hearing tests can give you some basic information, they cannot replace a full evaluation with an audiologist or hearing instrument specialist. 

Let’s take a closer look at what online tests can do, their limits, and why professional testing matters.

What Is an Online Hearing Test?

An online hearing test is usually done on a computer, tablet, or phone with headphones. You listen to different tones or spoken words at various volumes and then respond to what you hear. At the end, the program gives you results that may suggest whether you have some level of hearing loss.

These tests are simple and free, which makes them appealing. They can be helpful if you are just starting to notice changes in your hearing and want a quick check.

The Limits of Online Hearing Tests

Even though online tests can be a starting point, they are not very accurate. Here are a few reasons why:

Headphones and volume settings matter

If your headphones are not good quality or the volume is set too high or low, the results will not reflect your real hearing ability.

They don’t check the whole ear

A true hearing exam looks at your outer ear, middle ear, and inner ear. Online tests cannot tell if your hearing loss is caused by something simple, like earwax, or something more complex, like inner ear damage.

No one explains the results

A computer program might tell you there is a problem, but it cannot explain what kind of hearing loss you have or what steps to take next.

They don’t test speech clarity 

Hearing is not only about detecting sounds. It is also about understanding words. Online tests cannot fully measure how well you understand speech in quiet and noisy settings.

Why a Professional Hearing Test Matters

At Florida Medical Hearing, our hearing instrument specialist in Sebring and Lake Placid provides complete hearing evaluations. These tests go beyond simple tones. We use advanced tools to measure how your ears respond to sound and how well you understand speech.

A professional test can:

  • Find out the exact type and degree of hearing loss
  • Rule out simple issues like earwax buildup
  • Create a plan for treatment that fits your lifestyle and budget
  • Give you peace of mind by explaining the results clearly

With a full evaluation, you will not just know that you have hearing loss. You will know what kind it is, how serious it is, and what solutions will help you best.

Signs You Should Get a Hearing Test

If you are not sure whether you need a test, here are some signs to watch for:

  • You often ask people to repeat themselves
  • You feel like others are mumbling or speaking softly
  • It is hard to follow conversations in restaurants or group settings
  • You turn up the TV or radio louder than before
  • You hear ringing, buzzing, or hissing in your ears (tinnitus)
  • You feel more tired after social events because listening takes extra effort

If you notice any of these, an online test might confirm your concerns, but a professional exam will give you real answers.

Florida Medical Hearing performs hearing tests in Sebring, FL and Lake Placid, FL.

What to Expect During a Hearing Test

Some people put off getting tested because they feel nervous. But a hearing test is simple, safe, and painless. Here’s what happens during your visit at Florida Medical Hearing:

  1. Conversation – We talk about your health history and any concerns you have.
  2. Ear exam – We check inside your ears to look for blockages or other issues.
  3. Hearing test – You wear headphones and listen to tones and words at different volumes and pitches.
  4. Review results – We go over your audiogram (hearing chart) together and explain what it means.

The whole process usually takes less than an hour, and you’ll leave with a clear understanding of your hearing health.

Taking the Next Step

Online hearing tests can be a quick way to raise awareness, but they should not be your only step. Hearing loss is personal and unique, and the best care comes from a face-to-face evaluation with someone who understands your needs.

At Florida Medical Hearing, we are here to guide you. Whether you need hearing aids, custom ear protection, or just reassurance that your hearing is healthy, we will take the time to listen and provide care you can trust.

Contact us today to schedule your hearing test in Lake Placid, FL or Sebring, FL!

Home » Hearing » Understanding Pressure in Your Ear

Understanding Pressure in Your Ear

  • Debra Adair, HAS
  • October 2, 2025

Almost everyone has felt ear pressure at some point. It may feel like your ears are clogged, full, or under water. Some people describe it as having cotton stuffed inside their ear. This feeling can be uncomfortable and even distracting. While it often goes away on its own, ear pressure can sometimes point to a bigger issue that needs care.

At Florida Medical Hearing, our hearing instrument specialist in Sebring and Lake Placid, FL, helps people find answers for ear pressure and other ear problems. 

If ear pressure is getting in the way of your daily life, it is important to know what may be causing it and when to seek help.

What Causes Ear Pressure?

Ear pressure can come from many different reasons. Some of the most common include:

Changes in Air Pressure

If you have ever been on an airplane or driven up a mountain, you may have felt your ears “pop.” This happens because of a quick change in air pressure. Normally, your Eustachian tube (a small passage that connects your middle ear to the back of your throat) balances the pressure. But when it gets blocked or cannot keep up, you feel fullness or pressure.

Ear Infections

Ear infections are another common cause, especially in children but also in adults. An infection can lead to fluid buildup in the middle ear, which creates a heavy or full feeling. This may be followed by pain, muffled hearing, or even fever.

Earwax Blockage

Too much earwax can stop sound from passing through the ear canal normally. A buildup may make your ear feel plugged, cause ringing in the ears (tinnitus), or reduce hearing.

Allergies and Sinus Issues

Seasonal allergies, colds, or sinus infections can also create ear pressure. Swelling in your nasal passages can block your Eustachian tube, which stops your ear from equalizing pressure.

Other Possible Causes

  • Fluid in the ear from a cold
  • Jaw problems such as TMJ
  • Meniere’s disease (an inner ear disorder)
  • Sudden loud noise or barotrauma (damage from quick pressure changes)

Symptoms That May Come with Ear Pressure

Ear pressure is often more than just a heavy feeling. Other symptoms may include:

  • Muffled or reduced hearing
  • Dizziness or balance problems
  • Ear pain or sharp discomfort
  • Ringing or buzzing sounds
  • A popping or crackling feeling when swallowing or yawning

If these symptoms do not go away, it is a good idea to schedule a hearing test or ear exam.

When to See a Professional

Sometimes ear pressure is short-term and harmless, like during a flight. But if it lasts more than a few days or is joined by pain, drainage, or hearing loss, you should seek help. 

At Florida Medical Hearing, our hearing instrument specialist will check your ears, review your health history, and perform a full hearing evaluation if needed. This allows us to find out whether your ear pressure is from something simple, like earwax, or if it may need further medical care.

Treatment for Ear Pressure

Treatment depends on what is causing the problem. Some possible options include:

Earwax removal

A safe, gentle cleaning by a professional can relieve the pressure and restore hearing.

Hearing aids

If ear pressure is linked to hearing loss, properly fitted hearing aids may help.

Assistive devices

Sometimes, tools like tinnitus maskers or custom ear protection may reduce related symptoms.

Medical referral

If your ear pressure is due to infection, fluid buildup, or another condition, we will guide you to the right medical care.

How to Prevent Ear Pressure

While not all causes can be avoided, you can lower your chances of dealing with ear pressure by:

  • Using ear protection in loud environments
  • Managing allergies with proper care
  • Avoiding cotton swabs or sharp objects in the ear
  • Staying hydrated and chewing gum or swallowing during flights to help your ears adjust

Expert Hearing Care in Sebring, FL and Lake Placid, FL

Ear pressure can be uncomfortable and frustrating, but the good news is that help is available. Whether your ears feel clogged from wax, allergies, or another cause, we are here to help. Our hearing instrument specialist will take the time to listen to your concerns, check your ears, and guide you to the right solution.

Don’t let ear pressure keep you from enjoying conversations and daily life. 

Call us today to schedule an appointment and take the first step toward clearer, more comfortable hearing.

Are you ready to hear and be heard?

Hearing clearly impacts your quality of life now and for years to come in so many ways. Call us today and take the first step toward clearer, more confident listening.

What You Need to Know About Over-the-Counter Hearing Aids

  • Debra Adair, HAS
  • October 13, 2025

If you have been thinking about getting help for hearing loss, you may have seen ads for over-the-counter (OTC) hearing aids. These devices are sold in stores and online without a prescription. They are often less expensive than traditional hearing aids and can seem like a quick, simple fix.

But while OTC hearing aids may be helpful for some people, they are not the right choice for everyone. 

At Florida Medical Hearing, our hearing instrument specialist in Sebring and Lake Placid, FL helps people understand their options so they can make the best choice for their hearing health.

We also offer a wide selection of hearing aids to fit your lifestyle and budget.

What Are Over-the-Counter Hearing Aids?

Over-the-counter hearing aids are FDA-approved devices made for adults with mild to moderate hearing loss. You can buy them without a hearing test, doctor’s visit, or fitting. They are meant to make speech and everyday sounds easier to hear, especially in quiet places.

Unlike traditional hearing aids, OTC devices are not custom-programmed to your hearing test results. Instead, you usually set them up yourself with basic volume and tone controls or through a smartphone app.

Who Can Benefit From OTC Hearing Aids?

OTC hearing aids may help adults who:

  • Struggle to hear soft voices or conversations in quiet places
  • Notice mild hearing difficulties but can still manage without major problems
  • Want an affordable first step before trying professional hearing aids

If your hearing loss is more severe, if you have ringing in the ears (tinnitus), or if you often struggle in noisy places, OTC hearing aids may not give you the help you need.

How Are OTC Hearing Aids Different From Prescription Hearing Aids?

The main difference is personalization. Prescription hearing aids are fitted and programmed by a hearing instrument specialist based on a full hearing evaluation. This ensures the devices are tuned to your exact hearing needs, ear shape, and lifestyle.

OTC hearing aids are one-size-fits-most. They are designed to work in general situations, but they may not be able to handle complex hearing needs.

Prescription hearing aids also come with ongoing care. This includes fittings, adjustments, cleanings, and repairs to keep your devices working their best. OTC hearing aids do not include this level of support.

Are OTC Hearing Aids Safe?

Yes, OTC hearing aids are FDA-regulated, which means they meet safety standards. However, the risk is that people may try them when their hearing problem is caused by something else, like earwax buildup, an infection, or another medical condition. Without a professional exam, these issues can go untreated.

That is why we recommend starting with a hearing test at Florida Medical Hearing. Even if you decide to try OTC hearing aids, knowing your hearing health gives you peace of mind.

Why See an Audiologist or Hearing Instrument Specialist?

Choosing a hearing aid is not just about making sounds louder. It is about making speech clearer, reducing background noise, and making hearing more comfortable in daily life. A hearing instrument specialist can:

  • Test your hearing to confirm the type and level of loss
  • Rule out medical causes of hearing problems
  • Recommend whether OTC or prescription hearing aids are best for you
  • Fit and adjust devices so they feel comfortable and sound natural
  • Provide ongoing support for cleaning, repairs, and adjustments

With professional guidance, you are more likely to find a hearing solution that truly improves your quality of life.

When OTC Hearing Aids May Not Be Enough

You should seek professional care instead of using OTC hearing aids if you:

  • Have moderate to severe hearing loss
  • Struggle to follow conversations even in quiet places
  • Notice ringing, buzzing, or pain in your ears
  • Hear better in one ear than the other
  • Have sudden hearing loss

These signs may point to hearing needs that require more advanced solutions.

Hearing Instrument Specialists in Florida

If you are curious about over-the-counter hearing aids, they may be a starting point. But the best way to know what will help is to schedule a hearing test. 

At Florida Medical Hearing, we provide complete evaluations and explain your options clearly. Whether you need OTC devices, prescription hearing aids, or another solution, our hearing instrument specialist will guide you with care and honesty.

Don’t wait for hearing loss to get worse. 

Call Florida Medical Hearing today to schedule your hearing test in Sebring and Lake Placid, FL!

Are you ready to hear and be heard?

Hearing clearly impacts your quality of life now and for years to come in so many ways. Call us today and take the first step toward clearer, more confident listening.

How do I know if I need a hearing aid?

If you struggle to follow conversations (especially in noisy settings) turn up the TV or radio more often, or feel left out in social situations, it may be time to schedule a hearing evaluation. At Florida Medical Hearing, our hearing instrument specialist can check your hearing and let you know if hearing aids are the right option for you.

Can I get a hearing aid without a hearing test?

Yes. Over-the-counter (OTC) hearing aids are available without a hearing test and are designed for adults with mild to moderate hearing loss. However, a professional hearing evaluation at Florida Medical Hearing ensures your devices are properly matched to your hearing and that any underlying medical issues are not overlooked.

How long do hearing aids last?

Most hearing aids work well for 3 to 7 years, depending on the model, how often they’re used, and how well they’re cared for. Regular cleanings, safe storage, and routine check-ups with our hearing instrument specialist can help extend their life. Many patients also choose to upgrade sooner to take advantage of new technology and improved features.

PSAPs are simple amplifiers intended for people without hearing loss who just want to boost sound in certain situations. They don’t offer the same level of precision, comfort, or ongoing support that hearing aids do.

Home » Florida Medical Hearing Privacy Policy

Florida Medical Hearing Privacy Policy

This Privacy Policy describes how your personal information is collected, used, and shared when you visit or make a purchase from [https://www.floridamedicalhearingaids.com/] (the “Site”).

PERSONAL INFORMATION WE COLLECT

When you visit the Site, we automatically collect certain information about your device, including information about your web browser, IP address, time zone, and some of the cookies that are installed on your device. Additionally, as you browse the Site, we collect information about the individual web pages or products that you view, what websites or search terms referred you to the Site, and information about how you interact with the Site. We refer to this automatically collected information as “Device Information.”

We collect Device Information using the following technologies:

  • “Cookies” are data files that are placed on your device or computer and often include an anonymous unique identifier. For more information about cookies, and how to disable cookies, visit http://www.allaboutcookies.org.
  • “Log files” track actions occurring on the Site, and collect data including your IP address, browser type, Internet service provider, referring/exit pages, and date/time stamps. 
  • “Web beacons”, “tags”, and “pixels” are electronic files used to record information about how you browse the Site.

Additionally, when you make a purchase or attempt to make a purchase through the Site, we collect certain information from you, including your name, billing address, shipping address, payment information (including credit card numbers), email address, and phone number.  We refer to this information as “Order Information.”

When we talk about “Personal Information” in this Privacy Policy, we are talking about both Device Information and Order Information.

HOW DO WE USE YOUR PERSONAL INFORMATION?

We use the Order Information that we collect generally to fulfill any orders placed through the Site (including processing your payment information, arranging for shipping, and providing you with invoices and/or order confirmations).  Additionally, we use this Order Information to:

  • communicate with you;
  • screen our orders for potential risk or fraud; and
  • provide you with information or advertising relating to our products or services (depending on the preferences you have shared with us)

We use the Device Information that we collect to help us screen for potential risk and fraud (in particular, your IP address), and more generally to improve and optimize our Site (for example, by generating analytics about how our customers browse and interact with the Site), and to assess the success of our marketing and advertising campaigns.

SHARING YOUR PERSONAL INFORMATION

We may share your Personal Information with third parties as needed to help us use your Personal Information, as described above.  

We also use Google Analytics to help us understand how our customers use the Site–you can read more about how Google uses your Personal Information here:  https://www.google.com/intl/en/policies/privacy/.  You can also opt out of Google Analytics here:  https://tools.google.com/dlpage/gaoptout.

Finally, we may also share your Personal Information to comply with applicable laws and regulations, to respond to a subpoena, search warrant, or other lawful request for information we receive, or to otherwise protect our rights.

BEHAVIOURAL ADVERTISING

As described above, we may use your Personal Information to provide you with targeted advertisements or marketing communications we believe may be of interest to you.  For more information about how targeted advertising works, you can visit the Network Advertising Initiative’s (“NAI”) educational page at http://www.networkadvertising.org/understanding-online-advertising/how-does-it-work.

You can opt out of and/or adjust targeted advertising on:

  • Facebook by going to https://www.facebook.com/settings/?tab=ads
  • Google by going to https://www.google.com/settings/ads/anonymous
  • Bing by going to https://advertise.bingads.microsoft.com/en-us/resources/policies/personalized-ads

Additionally, you can opt out of some of these services by visiting the Digital Advertising Alliance’s opt-out portal at:  http://optout.aboutads.info/.

DO NOT TRACK

Please note that we do not alter our Site’s data collection and use practices when we see a Do Not Track signal from your browser.

DATA RETENTION

When you place an order through the Site, we will maintain your Order Information for our records unless and until you ask us to delete this information.

CHANGES

We may update this privacy policy from time to time in order to reflect, for example, changes to our practices or for other operational, legal, or regulatory reasons.

CONTACT US

For more information about our privacy practices, if you have questions, or if you would like to make a complaint, please contact us by e-mail at leads@proheargroup.com or by mail using the details provided below:

Sebring, FL

201 US Highway 27 South

Home » Phonak Hearing Aids

Phonak Hearing Aids

Florida Medical Hearing is an authorized provider of Phonak hearing aids in Sebring, FL and Lake Placid, FL.

At Florida Medical Hearing, we’re proud to fit and service Phonak hearing aids, one of the most trusted names in hearing technology worldwide. Phonak’s motto, “Well-Hearing is Well-Being,” reflects their belief that hearing your best helps you live your best life.

Phonak has a long history of creating hearing aids that make a real difference. With features like Bluetooth streaming, rechargeable batteries, water resistance, and outstanding speech clarity, their devices are built to meet the needs of people of all ages and lifestyles.

Whether you’re trying hearing aids for the first time or ready to upgrade to the latest technology, our hearing instrument specialists in Sebring and Lake Placid, FL will help you find the Phonak device that keeps you connected, comfortable, and confident.

Phonak Hearing Aids We Offer

Phonak Infinio Ultra Line

The Phonak Infinio Ultra Line represents the next evolution in hearing technology, delivering elevated clarity, efficiency, and adaptability through advanced AI processing. Released in October 2025, this flagship upgrade enhances the foundational Infinio platform with smarter sound recognition, better battery performance, and simplified maintenance. 

For many existing Phonak users, these improvements can be activated through an in-clinic firmware update, allowing current Infinio or Infinio Sphere devices to perform at an entirely new level without requiring new hardware.

Infinio Ultra R

A flexible, ultra-adaptive model powered by AutoSense OS™ 7.0, designed to automatically adjust to a wide range of environments. Ideal for patients seeking seamless performance in everyday listening.

Infinio Ultra Sphere

Engineered for complex and noisy situations, this model features Spheric Speech Clarity 2.0 and the breakthrough DEEPSONIC™ AI chip, the world’s first dedicated processor for real-time speech separation. This allows clearer understanding of multiple speakers from any direction, even in the most challenging soundscapes.

Unique Features:

  • AutoSense OS™ 7.0 adapts with 24% greater precision and is trained on 18× more sound scenarios, resulting in smarter environmental transitions and more natural listening.

  • Spheric Speech Clarity 2.0 (Ultra Sphere only) enhances speech up to threefold in noisy settings by isolating voices and reducing background interference.

  • Listening effort is reduced by up to 35%, supporting long-term comfort and decreasing auditory fatigue.

Phonak Virto Infinio

Phonak Virto Infinio is a custom in-the-ear (ITE) hearing aid released in 2025, built for those who want powerful performance in a discreet design. Each device is custom-molded to match the shape of your ear, giving you a snug, comfortable fit that lasts all day.

Packed with advanced sound technology, Virto Infinio delivers clear, natural hearing in a small, almost invisible shell, perfect for anyone who wants great sound without drawing attention.

Unique Features:

  • Custom-Made Fit: Each device is individually molded to the shape of your ear, ensuring a secure, comfortable fit that’s easy to wear all day.
  • AutoSense OS 6.0: Automatically adjusts settings based on your surroundings, providing seamless hearing whether you’re in quiet, noisy, or dynamic environments.
  • Durable & Water-Resistant: Rated IP68 for dust and moisture resistance, making it ideal for active lifestyles and daily wear.
  • Multiple Technology Levels: Available in four tiers (I90, I70, I50, I30) to meet a range of hearing needs and budgets.

Phonak Audéo Sphere

The Phonak Audéo Sphere delivers clear, natural sound in all kinds of listening environments. Whether you’re in a noisy restaurant, catching up with a friend, or enjoying your favorite music, it adapts automatically so listening feels easy and comfortable.

A standout feature of the Audéo Sphere is its all-day rechargeable battery. With just one charge, you’ll get reliable performance from morning until night-no more swapping tiny batteries. It also connects wirelessly to smartphones, letting you stream calls, music, and adjust settings through a simple app.

Unique Features:

  • Dedicated AI chip that separates speech from noise
  • Automatic sound adaptation for different environments
  • Bluetooth connectivity for streaming and app control

Phonak Audéo Infinio

The Phonak Audéo Infinio is designed to make everyday listening effortless. It improves speech recognition in noise, adjusts automatically to your surroundings, and pairs seamlessly with smartphones and other devices. This means you can enjoy clearer conversations with less listening fatigue.

The Audéo Infinio is slim, discreet, and available with a rechargeable battery option so you can wear it confidently all day.

Unique Features:

  • Works with CROS for single-sided hearing loss
  • RogerDirect™ ready for even better performance in noise and over distance
  • Connects to up to 8 Bluetooth devices, with 2 active at the same time

Phonak CROS Infinio

For people with single-sided deafness, the Phonak CROS Infinio is a life-changing solution. It wirelessly transmits sound from your non-hearing ear to your better ear, helping you stay aware of your surroundings and follow conversations naturally.

Lightweight and discreet, it pairs easily with compatible Phonak hearing aids to create a complete solution for single-sided hearing loss.

Unique Features:

  • Long-lasting rechargeable battery
  • Hands-free calls and streaming, even at greater distances
  • Universal Bluetooth connectivity

Phonak Virto Infinio

The Phonak Virto Infinio, released in 2025, is a custom in-the-ear (ITE) hearing aid made for people who want powerful sound in a nearly invisible design. Each device is molded to the shape of your ear, ensuring a snug, comfortable fit that lasts all day.

With advanced sound technology, Virto Infinio delivers clear, natural hearing in a tiny shell. It’s an excellent choice if you want strong performance without drawing attention to your hearing aids.

Unique Features:

  • Custom-made fit for comfort and security
  • AutoSense OS 6.0 adapts automatically to your surroundings
  • IP68 rating for water and dust resistance
  • Available in four technology levels (I90, I70, I50, I30)

Phonak Audéo Fit

The Phonak Audéo Fit combines great hearing with health tracking. Along with providing clear, natural sound, it measures your steps, distance, activity levels, wearing time, and even heart rate through the myPhonak app. You can view daily, weekly, and monthly health stats while enjoying reliable hearing throughout your day.

Unique Features:

  • Tracks steps, distance, activity, and heart rate in the myPhonak app
  • Tap Control for easy adjustments and call handling
  • Universal Bluetooth connectivity to phones, TVs, and Roger™ devices

Phonak Audéo Lumity

The Phonak Audéo Lumity uses AutoSense OS™ 5.0 with AI to adapt to your listening environment automatically. From quiet chats to noisy gatherings, it adjusts for natural, clear sound in every setting.

It’s available as a rechargeable receiver-in-the-ear (RITE) model, designed for people with mild to profound hearing loss. Plus, it’s the second generation of the world’s first waterproof rechargeable hearing aid, perfect for active lifestyles.

Unique Features:

  • Bluetooth connectivity to smartphones, TVs, and more
  • Customization through the myPhonak app
  • Remote Help for virtual adjustments when needed

Phonak Slim

The Phonak Slim delivers the same advanced tech as Audéo Lumity but with a modern, ergonomic design. Its slim shape fits comfortably behind the ear, even if you wear glasses or face masks, giving you both style and function.

Unique Features:

  • Rechargeable, all-day power
  • Bluetooth streaming and app controls
  • Discreet, ergonomic design for comfort and confidence

Phonak CROS Lumity

Made for single-sided hearing loss, the Phonak CROS Lumity sends sound from your non-hearing ear to your better ear. This helps you follow conversations no matter where the speaker is, giving you a seamless, immersive listening experience. It can be paired with several Phonak models, including Audéo L-R, Audéo L-RT, Naída L-PR, and Sky L-PR.

Unique Features:

  • Bluetooth streaming and app controls
  • Remote Help for virtual support
  • Compatible with multiple Phonak devices

Phonak Naída Lumity

The Phonak Naída Lumity is Phonak’s most powerful hearing aid, designed for people with severe to profound hearing loss. It scans your environment up to 700 times per second, giving you clear, detailed sound even in complex listening situations. It comes in a dependable behind-the-ear (BTE) style, providing both comfort and strong performance.

Unique Features:

  • Bluetooth connectivity and app control
  • Remote Help for support between visits
  • Powerful amplification with clear, natural sound

Phonak Sky Lumity

The Phonak Sky Lumity is specially designed for children, supporting learning and development with SmartSpeech™ Technology. This feature reduces background noise and makes speech clearer, helping kids stay engaged in school and everyday life. It comes in a BTE style, available as a rechargeable model or in an ultra-power (UP) version with a size 675 disposable battery.

Unique Features:

  • myPhonak Junior app for child-friendly controls and parent monitoring
  • Tamper-resistant options for child safety
  • Bluetooth connectivity for universal streaming

Phonak Virto Paradise

The Phonak Virto Paradise is a custom-fit, in-the-ear hearing aid designed for comfort, discretion, and natural sound. Each one is molded to your ear for a secure fit and almost invisible look. Built on Phonak’s Paradise platform, it enhances speech clarity in quiet and noisy settings. Plus, with Bluetooth streaming, you can connect to your phone, TV, and music easily.

Unique Features:

  • Custom-molded for a secure and discreet fit
  • Bluetooth connectivity with advanced pairing options
  • Control and personalization through the myPhonak app

Phonak Assistive Listening Devices (ALDs)

Phonak also offers Assistive Listening Devices that give you extra help in difficult listening situations. These can be used alongside your hearing aids or on their own.

  • Roger™ Wireless Microphones – Improve speech clarity in noise or at a distance by streaming a speaker’s voice directly to your hearing aids.
  • TV Connectors – Stream sound from your TV directly to your hearing aids for crystal-clear audio without raising the volume.
  • Bluetooth® Accessories – Make hands-free calls, listen to music, or stream audio from other devices straight to your hearing aids.
  • Remote Controls – Adjust volume, programs, or mute your devices with a simple remote or the myPhonak smartphone app.

Phonak offers additional accessories to enhance your hearing aid experience:

  • Table Mic II
  • Roger Focus II
  • Roger for Education
  • Clip on Mic
  • TV Connector
  • PartnerMic
  • Remote Control

Older Phonak Hearing Aids We Support

These hearing aid models may still be supported for hearing aid servicing and repair by Florida Medical Hearing In Sebring and Lake Placid, FL. Please contact us for more information.

  • Phonak Audéo Paradise
  • Phonak Naída Paradise
  • Phonak CROS Paradise
  • Phonak Naída Link Marvel
  • Phonak Sky Marvel
  • Phonak Audéo Marvel
  • Phonak Bolero B
  • Phonak Naída B
  • Phonak Virto B
  • Phonak Audéo B

Frequently Asked Questions About Phonak Hearing Aids

How long do Phonak hearing aids last?

On average, Phonak hearing aids last 5 to 7 years with proper care. Their lifespan depends on how often you use them, how well you clean them, and whether you keep up with regular checkups. Many people choose to upgrade sooner to enjoy the latest technology.

Are Phonak hearing aids rechargeable?

Yes, many Phonak models, such as the Audéo Lumity, Audéo Sphere, and Naída Lumity, come with rechargeable batteries. Most can last a full day on a single overnight charge, giving you reliable performance without the hassle of changing batteries.

How do Phonak hearing aids connect to phones and TVs?

Phonak hearing aids use Bluetooth technology to connect wirelessly to smartphones, TVs, and other devices. With the myPhonak app, you can stream phone calls, listen to music, and even adjust your hearing aid settings directly from your phone.

Find the Right Phonak Hearing Aid in Sebring and Lake Placid, FL

Florida Medical Hearing can help you find the Phonak hearing aid that fits your hearing needs and lifestyle. As an authorized Phonak provider in Sebring, FL and Lake Placid, FL, we offer the latest models, expert fittings, and personalized care.

Schedule your consultation with our hearing instrument specialist today!

Are you ready to hear and be heard?

Hearing clearly impacts your quality of life now and for years to come in so many ways. Call us today and take the first step toward clearer, more confident listening.

Oticon Hearing Aids

Florida Medical Hearing is an authorized provider of Oticon hearing aids in Sebring and Lake Placid, FL.

At Florida Medical Hearing, we’re proud to provide Oticon hearing aids, a brand trusted worldwide for more than 100 years. Oticon designs their devices with BrainHearing™ technology, which works with the way your brain naturally processes sound. This means you enjoy clearer speech, less effort when listening, and a sound that feels more natural.

Many Oticon models also include modern features like Bluetooth® streaming, rechargeable batteries, and tinnitus relief options, helping you stay connected, confident, and fully engaged in everyday life.

Oticon Hearing Aids in Sebring and Lake Placid, FL

Oticon Zeal

Oticon Zeal is the first hearing aid designed to do it all while staying completely unseen. Built for people who want powerful technology in an ultra-discreet design, Zeal delivers exceptional sound clarity, all-day comfort, and seamless connectivity in one tiny device no one will notice.

Zeal transforms the idea of what a hearing aid should be. Its small, nearly invisible design sits comfortably in your ear, giving you the confidence to live fully without drawing attention to your device. Powered by Oticon’s advanced BrainHearing™ technology, Zeal supports the brain’s natural way of processing sound. Enjoy clear, balanced, and comfortable hearing, even in noisy places.

Unique Features:

  • Made to Connect: Stream calls, meetings, music, and more directly from your phone, Apple Watch, TV, computer, or tablet. Zeal brings your digital world to life with smooth, high-quality wireless audio.
  • Reliable Rechargeability: No disposable batteries. With fast, dependable charging, you get all-day power that keeps up with your lifestyle.
  • Same-Day Fitting: Zeal is designed for convenience, offering the option for same-day fitting so you can begin hearing better right away.

Oticon Jet PX

The Oticon Jet PX is created for people with mild to severe hearing loss. It provides clear, natural sound that makes conversations easier to follow. One of the key features of the Jet PX is its wireless Bluetooth® connectivity, allowing you to stream phone calls, music, and TV audio directly into your hearing aids.

It also comes in a rechargeable option, giving you all-day use on a single charge so you don’t have to worry about changing batteries.

Key Features:

  • AI-powered OpenSound Navigator with DNN: Improves hearing with clear, natural sound.
  • Tinnitus SoundSupport: Ideal for users with tinnitus as it gives access to customizable relief sounds.
  • SuperShield: Quickly adapts and prevents feedback before it occurs.

Oticon Own SI

The Oticon Own SI is a custom in-the-ear hearing aid designed to fit your ear shape perfectly for all-day comfort and a discreet look. It is available in multiple styles, including options that sit deep in the ear canal and are nearly invisible.

Despite its small size, it’s packed with smart features like Bluetooth® connectivity for direct streaming from your smartphone, TV, or tablet.

Key Features:

  • Discreet and sophisticated: Super small styles (IIC and CIC) that fit fully in your ear
  • BrainHearing™ technology: Helps your brain make sense of sound.
  • Custom-fit: Built for all-day comfort, even with glasses or masks.
  • Strong and durable: Water- and dust-resistant to last through busy days.

Oticon Intent

The Oticon Intent is Oticon’s most advanced hearing aid yet, designed to deliver sound that feels natural and personal to you. It can detect your movements, surroundings, and even the way you engage in conversations.

This technology allows the device to adjust automatically, helping you hear more confidently in any listening environment.

Key Features:

  • Bluetooth® LE Audio for seamless, low-energy streaming
  • Self-Calibrating Speaker for optimized sound clarity
  • Fast Charging: 33% quicker recharge for all-day use

Oticon Real

The Oticon Real is built with BrainHearing™ technology, helping your brain process sound naturally. Vonversations feel easier to follow and listening takes less effort. What sets Oticon Real apart is its ability to handle sudden, loud, or disruptive sounds.

The Wind & Handling Stabilizer and SuddenSound Stabilizer features keep unexpected noises under control. Enjoy clearer speech, less distraction, and a more natural listening experience in any environment.

Key Features:

  • Bluetooth® streaming for iPhone® and Android™
  • App control via Oticon Companion or ON app
  • Tinnitus SoundSupport™ for calming tinnitus relief

Oticon Own

The Oticon Own is a custom in-the-ear (ITE) hearing aid designed to fit your ears snugly and offers a nearly invisible look. Each device is molded to the shape of your ear, so it stays secure, feels natural, and can be worn all day with confidence. It’s a great choice if you want powerful performance in a device that blends seamlessly into your lifestyle.

Key Features:

  • Bluetooth® compatibility with iPhone® and Android™
  • Remote adjustments via Oticon apps
  • Built-in tinnitus relief with SoundSupport™

Oticon Zircon

The Oticon Zircon brings the benefits of Oticon’s advanced technology to a budget-friendly hearing aid. It helps your brain process sound more naturally so you can enjoy clearer conversations and less listening fatigue. It is a smart choice if you want clearer sound and easy features at an approachable price.

Key Features:

  • Direct Bluetooth® streaming
  • Control through Oticon Companion and ON app
  • SoundSupport™ for tinnitus management

Oticon CROS

The Oticon CROS is designed for people with single-sided deafness (SSD). It picks up sounds from your non-hearing side and sending them wirelessly to a hearing aid on your better ear. It is lightweight, discreet, and comfortable for all-day wear.

The Oticon CROS helps you hear more naturally and follow important sounds and conversations no matter which side they come from.

The Oticon CROS is compatible with:

  • Oticon Real™
  • More™
  • Zircon™
  • Opn S™
  • Play PX™
  • Xceed™

Key Features:

  • TwinLink™ Bluetooth® for streaming and sound transmission
  • App-based remote control
  • Built-in tinnitus support

Oticon Play PX

The Oticon Play PX is the first pediatric hearing aid in the world to use deep neural network (DNN) technology, designed to work the same way the brain learns and understands sound. This advanced technology helps children hear speech more clearly, even in noisy places, while also reducing background noise.

By giving kids access to a full range of sounds, Play PX supports speech development, language learning, and everyday communication. It’s built to help children explore their world with confidence, connect with others, and thrive as they grow.

Key Features:

  • Tamper-resistant battery doors
  • Water & dust resistance (IP68-rated)
  • LED indicators for battery status
  • Bluetooth® streaming to smartphones and tablets

Accessories for Kids:

  • EduMic – A teacher-worn mic for classroom clarity
  • Amigo FM – Enhances understanding in noisy settings
  • ConnectClip – Wireless streaming up to 65 feet

Oticon Xceed

The Oticon Xceed is one of the most powerful hearing aids available, designed for people with severe to profound hearing loss. Built with Oticon’s BrainHearing™ technology, it helps you hear speech more clearly while reducing background noise.

Xceed gives you access to the full sound picture, making conversations easier to follow and daily life more enjoyable. With Bluetooth® streaming, tinnitus support, and rechargeable options.

Key Features:

  • Bluetooth® streaming for calls and media
  • Real-time adjustments via app
  • Tinnitus relief with SoundSupport™
  • Available in rechargeable and disposable models

Assistive Listening Devices & Technology by Oticon

  • ConnectClip
  • Remote Control
  • Streamer Pro
  • TV Adapter

Older Oticon Hearing Aids We Support

The hearing aid models below may still be supported for hearing aid servicing and repair by Florida Medical Hearing in Sebring and Lake Placid, FL. Please contact us for more information.

  • Oticon Opn
  • Oticon Opn Play
  • Oticon Opn S
  • Oticon Ruby
  • Oticon Siya
  • Oticon More

If you have an older Oticon model and need support, we’re here to help!

Frequently Asked Questions About Oticon Hearing Aids

What is the average lifespan of an Oticon hearing aid?

The average lifespan of an Oticon hearing aid is about 5 to 7 years with proper care. Regular cleanings, routine check-ups, and safe storage can help extend the life of your devices.

What is BrainHearing™ technology in Oticon hearing aids?

BrainHearing™ is Oticon’s unique technology that helps your brain make sense of sound more easily. Instead of just making sounds louder, it supports natural sound processing, which can lead to clearer speech and less listening fatigue.

How to connect Oticon hearing aids to Bluetooth devices?

You can connect Oticon hearing aids to Bluetooth devices by using the Oticon Companion app or following the pairing instructions in your user guide. Once paired, you can stream phone calls, music, and TV audio directly to your hearing aids.

Find the Right Oticon Hearing Aid in Sebring, FL and Lake Placid, FL

Florida Medical Hearing can help you find the Oticon hearing aid that fits your hearing needs and lifestyle. As an authorized Oticon provider in Lake Placid, FL and Sebring, FL, we offer the latest models, expert fittings, and personalized care.

Schedule your consultation with our hearing instrument specialist today!

Are you ready to hear and be heard?

Hearing clearly impacts your quality of life now and for years to come in so many ways. Call us today and take the first step toward clearer, more confident listening.

ReSound Hearing Aids

ReSound Hearing Aids in Sebring, FL and Lake Placid, FL

ReSound Enzo IA

With a stylish, durable design, the ReSound Enzo IA is built for the most challenging listening situations. It offers exceptional clarity, feedback control, and all-day battery life.

ReSound Nexia

The Unitron Smile is Unitron’s latest and most advanced hearing aid platform, launched in 2025. It’s designed to make listening feel effortless and natural. Hearing aids built on the Smile platform are easy to hear with, easy to wear, and simple to use.

What are the levels of technology in Unitron?

Unitron offers hearing aids in four main technology levels, each with different features to match your needs:

  • Level 5 – Standard
  • Level 7 – Advanced
  • Level 9 – Premium

The higher the level, the more advanced the hearing aid is at reducing noise, adapting to your surroundings, and making speech easier to understand in difficult environments.

You can stream with Unitron hearing aids by connecting them to your Bluetooth®-enabled device, such as a smartphone, tablet, or TV accessory. Most modern Unitron models use Made For All (MFA) Bluetooth technology, which allows them to pair directly with iPhones, Android phones, and other devices.

Widex Moment BTE

The Widex Moment is designed to deliver sound that feels as close to natural hearing as possible. Using advanced ZeroDelay™ technology, it processes sound instantly, so what you hear matches real life without the lag or distortion found in many other hearing aids. This hearing aid is available in several styles, including discreet receiver-in-canal (RIC) and behind-the-ear (BTE) options, so you can choose the look and fit that works best for you.

With Bluetooth® streaming, you can connect to your smartphone, TV, or tablet for calls, music, and entertainment. The Widex Moment is a great choice for people with mild to severe hearing loss who want a comfortable, modern device that provides clear, lifelike sound in everyday situations.

Key Features:

  • Bluetooth connectivity supports streaming from smartphones and other devices
  • Sound settings can be customized through the Moment app
  • Zen therapy offers integrated tinnitus relief options
  • Available with rechargeable or traditional size 13 batteries

Widex Moment CROS

The Widex Moment CROS is built for people with single-sided hearing loss or single-sided deafness (SSD). It works by sending sound wirelessly from your non-hearing ear to your better-hearing ear, so you can pick up speech and sounds from all directions. This makes conversations easier to follow, even in noisy places or when someone is talking on your weaker side.

What makes the Widex Moment CROS stand out is its clear, natural sound powered by Widex’s advanced technology. With its slim, discreet design, it’s comfortable for all-day wear and blends in effortlessly with your daily routine. The Widex Moment CROS can be paired with compatible Widex hearing aids, giving you a full solution for one-sided hearing loss that helps you stay connected and confident in every situation.

Key Features:

  • Can be configured for both CROS and BiCROS setups
  • Designed to be energy efficient with lower power consumption
  • Integrates easily with other Widex Moment hearing aids

Widex Moment ITE

The Widex Moment ITE (In-the-Ear) hearing aid is custom-made to fit the unique shape of your ear, giving you a comfortable and secure fit that feels natural all day long. Sitting inside the ear, it offers a discreet style while still delivering the clear, lifelike sound Widex is known for.

With advanced sound processing, the Widex Moment ITE helps you follow conversations with less effort, whether you’re at home, at work, or out with friends. It also offers wireless connectivity options, so you can stream calls, music, and media straight to your hearing aids.

Key Features:

  • Custom-fit design ensures a secure and personalized fit
  • Supports Bluetooth streaming and app-based adjustments
  • Powered by size 10 or 312 disposable batteries with up to 150 hours of use
  • Offers built-in Zen Tinnitus Therapy for soothing soundscapes

Widex Assistive Listening Accessories

Take your Widex hearing experience further with accessories designed to improve communication and convenience:

  • Widex TV Play streams high-quality audio directly from your television to your hearing aids
  • Widex Sound Assist functions as a microphone, remote control, and hands-free calling device
  • Com-Dex, RC-Dex, and TV-Dex provide additional ways to control your hearing aids and stay connected

Older Widex Models We Support

At Florida Medical Hearing, we continue to service and repair a range of older Widex hearing aids, including:

  • Widex Evoke
  • Widex Beyond
  • Widex Unique
  • Widex Dream

If you have an older model, contact us to see how we can help maintain or repair your device.

Frequently Asked Questions – Widex Hearing Aids

What is the average lifespan of a Widex hearing aid?

The average lifespan of a Widex hearing aid is about 5 to 7 years with proper care. How long your devices last will depend on how often you use them, how well you maintain them, and whether you update as new technology becomes available.

How to connect Widex hearing aids to a smartphone?

You can connect Widex hearing aids to your smartphone through Bluetooth®. For iPhone, pairing is done directly in the phone’s settings. For Android, pairing may require the Widex Moment app. Once connected, you can stream calls, music, and videos directly to your hearing aids.

What is Widex Zen Therapy?

Widex Zen Therapy is a built-in feature in many Widex hearing aids designed to help with tinnitus. It plays gentle, relaxing tones or soundscapes that can make ringing or buzzing in the ears less noticeable and more manageable throughout the day.

Find the Right Widex Hearing Aid in Sebring, FL and Lake Placid, FL

At Florida Medical Hearing, we’re more than happy to help you find the Widex hearing aid that’s right for your hearing needs, lifestyle, and budget. As an authorized Widex provider in Sebring, FL and Lake Placid, FL, we offer the latest models, personalized fittings, and long-term support.

Contact us today to schedule a consultation and experience what Widex technology can do for your hearing!

Hearing clearly impacts your quality of life now and for years to come in so many ways. Call us today and take the first step toward clearer, more confident listening.

data.title: Ear Candling Risks: Why It's Not Safe | Florida Medical Hearing Aids
data.meta_description: Learn the risks of ear candling, why ear candles are not recommended for wax removal, safer options, and when to have your ears checked.
data.h1: Ear Candling Risks: Why Ear Candles Are Not a Safe Way to Remove Wax
data.body:
Home » Hearing » Ear Candling Risks: Why Ear Candles Are Not a Safe Way to Remove Wax

Ear Candling Risks: Why Ear Candles Are Not a Safe Way to Remove Wax

  • Debra Adair, HAS
  • April 14, 2026
  • Ear candling is often promoted as a natural way to remove ear wax, but it can cause burns, wax blockage, ear canal injury, and delayed care.
  • Safer ear care starts with knowing when wax is actually a problem and when a professional ear exam is the better next step.
  • Florida Medical Hearing helps patients understand ear candling risks while providing safer hearing care options in Sebring and Lake Placid, FL.

Ear candling may sound simple: place a hollow candle in the ear, light the end, and let heat or suction pull wax out. The problem is that ear candles do not reliably remove ear wax, and they can create new problems inside or around the ear.

People often search for ear candling risks because they are trying to solve a blocked, clogged, itchy, or muffled ear. That concern is understandable. Ear wax buildup can affect comfort and hearing. But using fire, hot wax, and a cone-shaped candle near the ear canal is not a safe way to treat it.

If you are worried about wax, hearing changes, pressure, or discomfort, the safest step is to understand what ear candling can do, what it cannot do, and when to have your ears checked.

What Is Ear Candling?

Ear candling, sometimes called ear coning, uses a hollow cone or tube made from fabric coated in wax. One end is placed near or slightly into the ear canal, and the other end is lit.

Supporters claim the candle creates suction that pulls ear wax and debris from the ear. Some also claim it can help with sinus pressure, tinnitus, headaches, or general wellness. These claims are not supported by good evidence.

The dark material left inside the candle after burning is often described as ear wax, but it can come from the candle itself. Seeing residue does not prove that wax was removed from the ear.

Is Ear Candling Safe?

No. Ear candling is not considered a safe method for removing ear wax. Health organizations warn against it because the process involves an open flame close to the face and ear, plus the risk of hot wax or ash entering the ear canal.

The ear canal and eardrum are delicate. Even a small burn, scrape, blockage, or pressure injury can cause pain, infection, temporary hearing loss, or more serious damage.

Ear candling is especially risky for children, older adults, people with ear tubes, anyone with a hole in the eardrum, and anyone with a history of ear surgery or ear infections.

What Are the Main Ear Candling Risks?

Ear candling risks include more than simple irritation. The most common concerns involve heat, wax, injury, and delayed diagnosis.

Burns

Ear candles use a flame. That creates a burn risk for the outer ear, face, hair, scalp, ear canal, and eardrum. Burns near the ear can be painful and may require medical care.

Candle Wax in the Ear

Hot candle wax can drip into the ear canal. If it hardens, it may create a new blockage or sit against the eardrum. This can make hearing feel muffled and may require professional removal.

Eardrum Injury

The eardrum is thin and sensitive. Ear candling may contribute to eardrum irritation, puncture, or other trauma, especially if the candle is inserted too far or if wax, ash, or pressure affects the canal.

Ear Canal Scratches or Infection

The ear canal skin can be easily irritated. Scratches, burns, or trapped debris can increase the chance of infection. Pain, swelling, drainage, or worsening fullness after candling should be checked.

Temporary Hearing Loss

Hearing may become worse if candle wax, ash, or existing ear wax blocks the ear canal. Temporary hearing loss can also happen when the ear canal becomes swollen or irritated.

Delayed Care for a Different Problem

Not every clogged ear is caused by wax. Fluid behind the eardrum, swimmer’s ear, eustachian tube dysfunction, sudden sensorineural hearing loss, tinnitus, and other conditions can feel similar. Trying ear candling first may delay the right care.

Does Ear Candling Actually Remove Ear Wax?

There is no good evidence that ear candling reliably removes ear wax. The idea that the candle creates enough suction to pull wax from the ear canal does not hold up well.

Ear wax also does not usually need to be removed unless it is causing symptoms, blocking the ear canal, affecting a hearing test, interfering with hearing aids, or preventing a provider from seeing the eardrum.

When wax does need to be removed, safer options are available. The right option depends on your ear health, symptoms, and medical history.

Why Do Ears Feel Better or Worse After Candling?

Some people believe candling helps because the ear briefly feels warm, dry, or less full. That sensation does not prove that wax was removed.

Others feel worse after candling. That can happen if candle wax enters the ear, if the canal becomes irritated, if existing wax gets pushed deeper, or if the real issue was never ear wax in the first place.

If your ear feels blocked, painful, wet, itchy, or muffled after ear candling, do not put more products or tools into the ear.

Schedule an ear check so the canal and eardrum can be examined.

What Should You Do Instead of Ear Candling?

If your ear feels clogged or you think wax is affecting your hearing, start with a safer approach.

  • Avoid putting candles, cotton swabs, hair pins, earbuds, or other objects into the ear canal.
  • Do not try ear candling if you have pain, drainage, ear tubes, a known eardrum perforation, or a history of ear surgery.
  • Consider wax-softening drops only if you are confident they are safe for your ears and you have no warning symptoms.
  • Schedule an ear exam if your hearing feels blocked, symptoms are one-sided, or home care does not help.
  • Get a hearing test if the ear canal is clear but hearing still feels reduced.

The safest plan is to first confirm whether wax is actually present. A provider can look inside the ear and determine whether wax removal, hearing testing, or medical referral is needed.

When Should You Get Your Ear Checked?

You should have your ear checked if symptoms are painful, sudden, one-sided, or not improving. This is especially important because wax is only one possible cause of hearing changes.

Schedule an evaluation if you notice:

  • Sudden hearing loss
  • A clogged or blocked feeling that does not clear
  • Ear pain
  • Drainage or bleeding
  • Ringing that starts suddenly
  • Dizziness or spinning vertigo
  • Wax or debris stuck in the ear after candling
  • A history of eardrum problems, ear tubes, or ear surgery
  • Hearing aids that suddenly sound weak even after cleaning

If a hearing change is sudden, do not assume it is wax. Sudden hearing loss should be treated as time-sensitive.

Safer Ways to Manage Ear Wax

Ear wax helps protect and lubricate the ear canal. In many cases, the ear moves wax outward naturally, and the only cleaning needed is wiping the outer ear with a soft cloth.

When wax becomes excessive or impacted, safer options may include professional removal, careful use of wax-softening drops, or a provider-guided cleaning plan. Professional removal may involve visual inspection and tools or techniques selected for your ear condition.

People who wear hearing aids may need periodic ear checks because hearing aids can make wax buildup more noticeable. Wax can also clog hearing aid parts, which may make the device sound weak even when your hearing has not changed.

Common Questions About Ear Candling Risks

Can ear candling damage your hearing?

Yes, it can. Hearing may be affected if candle wax blocks the ear canal, if the canal becomes swollen or injured, or if the eardrum is irritated or damaged. If hearing changes after candling, have the ear checked.

Can ear candles push wax deeper?

Ear candling may leave residue or debris in the ear, and attempts to clean afterward can push wax deeper. Any object placed near or inside the canal can make a blockage worse if used incorrectly.

Is the residue inside an ear candle really ear wax?

Not necessarily. The residue can come from the candle materials as they burn. Seeing dark residue inside the candle does not prove that ear wax was removed from your ear.

Are ear candles safe if someone else does it for you?

No. Having another person hold the candle does not remove the risks of burns, dripping wax, canal injury, eardrum injury, or delayed care.

What if I already tried ear candling?

If you feel fine and have no symptoms, avoid repeating it. If you have pain, fullness, muffled hearing, drainage, ringing, dizziness, or concern that wax has entered the ear, schedule an ear exam.

Ear Candling Isn’t Worth the Risk – Here’s Your Next Step

Ear candling is not a safe or reliable way to remove ear wax. It can cause burns, wax blockage, ear canal injury, eardrum problems, temporary hearing changes, and delays in getting the right diagnosis.

If your ear feels clogged or your hearing has changed, a safer next step is an ear check.
